ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-24-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Scott Bassin for Ronald Althaus (Administrator).
Jonathan Madison for Jeffrey Panzer (Former Administrator).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not Fried & Williams, and Mr. Panzer are entitled to extraordinary fees and additional statutory fees, whether the releases signed in the settlement agreement regarding the fire case waive fees in the Estate, whether it is double dipping, whether Fried & Williams are entitled to fees for a time period after their Client was no longer the Administrator, whether the Management Agreement entitles Mr. Panzer to additional fees, whether or not it is appropriate for fees regarding an indemnity claim or contract claim to be requested in the Estate, etc. The Petition for Final Distribution and Payment of Remaining Statutory Fees is under submission. The Court informed Mr. Bassin that it needs a revised order with a blank for the statutory fees and a blank for the extraordinary fees.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-03-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott Bassin for petitioner Ronald Althus; Clinton Killian for former administrator Jeffrey Panzer.

Mr. Bassin reported that there were still some matters to finalize before final distribution, and requested that the Court go forward with a preliminary distribution. The Court inquired whether a &$500,000 reserve would be sufficient to cover the remaining liabilities of the estate. Mr. Bassin responded that the insurer is providing a defense to the claims by the tenant, and that the carrier believes that $500,000 would be the maximum potential recovery. To be on the safe side, his client would agree to a higher reserve hold back, and suggested that $1,000,000.00 would be quite adequate. Mr. Killian had no objection to the proposed preliminary distribution with a $1,000,000.00 hold back. The Court inquired if mediation of the remaining dispute would be appropriate. Mr. Killian said that he believes mediation would be helpful. Mr. Bassin expressed the concern that his client might view mediation as unnecessary delay. The Court strongly encouraged the parties to pursue mediation, and Mr. Bassin stated that he would strongly encourate his client to mediate. The Court stated that he would grant approval of a preliminary distribution subject to a written order, and requested a revised order providing for distribution of the real property and all but $1,000,000 of the cash, and Mr. Bassin agreed to submit the revised order. The Court set a status hearing re filing a petition for final distribution or petition to approve status report for May 10, 2017, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott Bassin for Ronald Althaus. Clinton Killian for (administrator) J.J. Panzer. Janet Boessenecker for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court stated that we are here on the administrator's petition to approve the accounting, Mr. Panzer's petition to remove the administrator, that various issues have been raised, that there are a lot of issues raised by the examiner regarding the accounting presented and asked the parties if they have talked to each other regarding how to resolve the issues. Mr. Killian stated that they started discussion last night, that a short continuance may resolve the matters and that new lawsuits have been filed. Mr. Bassin stated that they are not sure regarding the waiver of accounting, that if the petition gets approved they will not need to step in, that the lawsuits mentioned do not name the estates and that in principle they agree to discussions. The Court stated that it is inclined to continue the matter for 30 days, that the parties should continue to talk, that the accounting as it stands is not in a condition to approve, that it is in the parties interest to figure out how to resolve, and if not resolved strongly encourages the administrator to address the issues. Mr. Killian stated that they filed a response but have not received notification of acceptance from e-file yet. The Court stated that the Public Administrator did not have the estate long. The accounting petition and the petition for removal of administrator and appointment of successor administrator was continued to April 5, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Jun-11-2014 Continued To Aug-20-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Clinton Killian for Jeffrey Panzer.

The Court expressed concern about a Special Needs Trust ("SNT") for the Decedent's Mother and stated that it has not seen the Trust document. Mr. Killian stated that the Trust is a private document, that he did not want to file it, that the assets will be used for the Mother's maintenance and care, and that the Mother is 88 years of age. The Court informed Mr. Killian that you can not have a Special Needs Trust for someone over age 65 and that they must have a pooled Trust. Mr. Killian stated the Guardian through the Texas Court established the Trust, that the three Children of the Brother will be beneficiaries, and that they want to have the art, automobile and the furnishings transferred to the SNT so that it can be efficiently liquidated. Mr. Killian also stated that the Schwab account is a mutual fund like account, that they will receive cash if they close it, that there is no ownership of stock and that he will talk to the Referee about changing the inventory. The Court asked why the art, etc. can't be liquidated in the Estate, expressed concern about self dealings, stated that it is not it is willing to waive an accounting, that it can't distribute to a SNT, that it needs to be a pooled Trust and that a petition for approval of the first account is needed. The accounting petition was continued to August 20, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
